content: {"summary": "Transit-discovered planets dominate the sample (68%) and cluster at short orbital periods (all < 9 days) with radii from 0.85 to 15 Earth radii. Radial velocity detections span a much wider period range (up to 5218 days) but have higher radius NULL rates. Imaging finds only distant giants with periods of thousands to hundreds of thousands of days. This confirms strong method-dependent selection effects that must be accounted for in any cross-method analysis.", "view_requested": null}
